entries.claim_text: The distinct training types/stages of modern LLMs and what each stage changes
entries.quoted_snippet: Training Type of LLM

entries.claim_text: This note is a survey answering a cross-paper question, hence Venue Kind = survey
entries.quoted_snippet: Instruction Tuning for Large Language Models: A Survey

entries.claim_text: Sources table lists the four primary papers by title
entries.quoted_snippet: Direct Preference Optimization

entries.claim_text: LoRA source row title
entries.quoted_snippet: LoRA: Low-Rank Adaptation of Large Language Models

entries.claim_text: Published venues (NeurIPS 2022, ICLR 2022, NeurIPS 2023) are author prior knowledge, not verified in the fetched arXiv full text
entries.quoted_snippet: instruction tuning

entries.claim_text: Instruction tuning is supervised re-training on (instruction, output) pairs that bridges the next-word prediction objective and instruction following
entries.quoted_snippet: bridges the gap between the next-word prediction objective of LLMs

entries.claim_text: InstructGPT SFT dataset size used in the audited taxonomy table
entries.quoted_snippet: SFT dataset contains about 13k training prompts

entries.claim_text: LoRA parameter-efficiency figure used in the audited taxonomy table
entries.quoted_snippet: 10,000 times

entries.claim_text: Instruction-tuning compute is a small fraction of pre-training
entries.quoted_snippet: fine-tuning costs 0.2

entries.claim_text: SFT/instruction tuning yields more controllable and predictable behavior; Fine-tuning and Instruct-tuning collapse to one SFT stage
entries.quoted_snippet: allows for a more controllable and predictable model behavior compared to standard LLMs

entries.claim_text: Superficial alignment hypothesis: knowledge/capabilities almost all acquired in pre-training; alignment teaches user-preferred formalizations
entries.quoted_snippet: the knowledge and capabilities of a model are almost acquired in the pre-training stage

entries.claim_text: The survey records the criticism that SFT captures surface-level patterns/styles rather than comprehending tasks
entries.quoted_snippet: captures surface-level patterns and styles (e.g., the output format) rather than comprehending and learning

entries.claim_text: Labelers prefer 1.3B InstructGPT over 175B GPT-3; same architecture, differs only by human-data fine-tuning
entries.quoted_snippet: outputs from the 1.3B parameter InstructGPT model are preferred to outputs from the 175B GPT-3, despite having over 100x fewer parameters

entries.claim_text: InstructGPT improves truthfulness and reduces toxic output
entries.quoted_snippet: improvements in truthfulness and reductions in toxic output generation

entries.claim_text: InstructGPT alignment is three steps: SFT, reward model training, PPO
entries.quoted_snippet: reinforcement learning via proximal policy optimization (PPO)

entries.claim_text: InstructGPT uses a 6B reward model to save compute and stabilize RL
entries.quoted_snippet: In this paper we only use 6B RMs

entries.claim_text: DPO optimizes a policy with a simple binary cross-entropy objective
entries.quoted_snippet: optimize a policy using a simple binary cross entropy objective

entries.claim_text: DPO optimizes without an explicit standalone reward model or sampling from the policy during training
entries.quoted_snippet: without learning an explicit, standalone reward model or sampling from the policy during training

entries.claim_text: DPO loss is a log-sigmoid of beta-scaled log-ratio differences between preferred and dispreferred completions
entries.quoted_snippet: -\mathbb{E}_{(x, y_w, y_l)\sim \mathcal{D}}\left[\log \sigma \left(\beta \log \frac

entries.claim_text: DPO implicitly optimizes the same KL-constrained reward-maximization objective as RLHF (the model is secretly a reward model)
entries.quoted_snippet: implicitly optimizes the same objective as existing RLHF algorithms (reward maximization with a KL-divergence constraint)

entries.claim_text: DPO win rate approximately 61% at temperature 0.0 on TL;DR, edging PPO's ~57%
entries.quoted_snippet: win rate of approximately 61

entries.claim_text: LoRA freezes pretrained weights and injects trainable rank-decomposition matrices into each Transformer layer
entries.quoted_snippet: freezes the pre-trained model weights and injects trainable rank decomposition matrices into each layer of the Transformer architecture

entries.claim_text: LoRA reparameterizes the weight update as a low-rank product BA added to the frozen W0
entries.quoted_snippet: W_0+\Delta W=W_0+BA

entries.claim_text: LoRA reduces trainable params 10,000x and GPU memory 3x on GPT-3 175B
entries.quoted_snippet: LoRA can reduce the number of trainable parameters by 10,000 times and the GPU memory requirement by 3 times

entries.claim_text: LoRA performs on-par or better than fine-tuning on RoBERTa/DeBERTa/GPT-2/GPT-3
entries.quoted_snippet: performs on-par or better than fine-tuning in model quality on RoBERTa, DeBERTa, GPT-2, and GPT-3

entries.claim_text: Merging BA into W introduces no additional inference latency
entries.quoted_snippet: we do not introduce any additional latency during inference

entries.claim_text: The 1.3B>175B result shows alignment/behavior gains but there is a measured capability cost (alignment tax); the 'zero-cost, interface-only' framing is refuted
entries.quoted_snippet: comes at the cost of lower performance on certain tasks that we may care about

entries.claim_text: Regressions reduced by mixing PPO updates with pretraining log-likelihood updates (PPO-ptx)
entries.quoted_snippet: mixing PPO updates with updates that increase the log likelihood of the pretraining distribution

entries.claim_text: The four sources span different eras/models/metrics; none is a head-to-head on current-generation models, so the composite map is a cross-era montage
entries.quoted_snippet: 1.3B parameter InstructGPT model are preferred to outputs from the 175B GPT-3

entries.claim_text: Preference metrics risk self-defined targets; DPO itself notes ROUGE can be poorly correlated with human preferences, so 'better' is relative to the evaluator's preference
entries.quoted_snippet: automatic evaluation metrics such as ROUGE can be poorly correlated with human preferences

entries.claim_text: LoRA is a resource-side method, not a stage; naming instruction tuning vs SFT as separate columns is repackaging, not a real pipeline split
entries.quoted_snippet: we do not introduce any additional latency during inference

entries.claim_text: The 'no new knowledge, interface-only' folk claim is only half-supported: superficial alignment supports the knowledge half but is an unreplicated hypothesis, while alignment tax refutes the zero-cost half
entries.quoted_snippet: the knowledge and capabilities of a model are almost acquired in the pre-training stage
